purpose: The COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi repository provides functionality for managing loan selling operations between Marketplace Lenders (MPLs) and investors. It handles the purchase of loans, fee collections, interest calculations, and reconciliation processes within Cross River Bank's lending ecosystem.
businessFeatures: Loan grooming and preparation for sale
Volume fee collection and monthly minimum requirements
Reporting for pre-sale and post-sale loans

apis.purpose: Initiates the purchase of loans from MPLs
Generates pre-sale loan reports for MPLs and investors
Generates post-sale loan reports for processed batches
Completes a batch of loan purchases
Prepares loans for sale by grooming them to meet requirements

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Represents a loan to be purchased from an MPL

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Represents an action performed on a loan (purchase, fee collection, interest accrual)

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Represents a money transfer between accounts for various operations

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Defines the agreement terms between MPL and bank, including fee structures

dataEntities.name: Account
dataEntities.description: Represents various accounting accounts involved in loan transactions

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Represents a batch of loans processed together

dataEntities.name: VolumeFee
dataEntities.description: Represents fees collected based on loan volume

messagingPatterns.name: TransferOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Ensures reliable processing of transfers by storing them before execution

messagingPatterns.name: DailyInterestOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages accrual of daily interest on loans

messagingPatterns.name: FeeOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages the collection of various fees

messagingPatterns.name: NotificationOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages sending notifications to external systems

messagingPatterns.name: ReportingOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages the generation and storage of reports

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Message Queue
messagingPatterns.description: Handles asynchronous processing of messages between system components

findings.title: Embedded SQS credentials in code
findings.description: The application appears to have AWS SQS access credentials in environment variables or configuration. This creates security risk if these credentials are ever exposed. Recommended to use IAM roles or secure credential management.

findings.title: Missing error handling in outbox processors
findings.description: Several outbox processors lack comprehensive error handling which could lead to message loss or processing failures. Implement proper retry mechanisms and dead letter queues for all processors.

findings.title: Inefficient batch processing patterns
findings.description: Current implementation processes loans in batches but does not optimize database operations for large batches, which could lead to performance issues with large loan volumes. Implement chunked processing with optimized database access.
